title: Asa's Enchanted Books
description: Aiming to give every enchanted book a unique texture while attempting to emulate the vanilla style.
body:
![Asa's Enchanted Books v1.0](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4e98ab1446380840a2f4af1586dbca3a6a33127b.png)

I've always loved the vanilla textures of Minecraft. However, I always thought it would be cool if certain repetitive items had unique textures, with enchanted books being one of these items. So I set out to make a unique texture for every enchanted book how I imagine Mojang might some day. This pack attempts to make every enchanted book feel unique while emulating the vanilla art style. I have been using this pack playing casually while developing it and it has certainly enhanced my immersion and gameplay experience. I hope it will do the same for you.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/xbHuMYWT0jg" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## Identification
I attempted to make a predictable and understandable pattern for how the books look in their most basic form. These traits are observable in almost every enchanted book barring a few exceptions that break the pattern slightly for aesthetic purposes.

### Enchanted Book Level
All book textures have slightly altered variants meant to display the level of the enchantment applied to the book. As the level of the enchantment grows a badge on the ribbon of the book will change to a higher tier mimicking the appearance of the badges villagers use to display their profession level. Additionally, the higher the enchantment value of the book, the larger the book will appear.

![This image is titled “Book Levels”. Five books are displayed in the image. Starting with the left-most book and progressing towards the right, the first book is displayed as a level one enchanted book. This book looks similar to a normal unenchanted book with no added features. The next book is displayed as a level two enchanted book. This book looks similar to the first one, however, it is now equipped with a ribbon and an iron colored badge. The next book is displayed as a level three enchanted book. This book looks more similar to the second one, it is depicted as slightly larger than the last two and the badge is now gold colored. The next book is displayed as a level four enchanted book. This book is the same size as the third book, additionally, this book now has two ribbons while the last two books each only had one, there are now two badges on this book with one residing on each ribbon, both badges are emerald colored. The final book is displayed as a level five enchanted book. This book looks most similar to the fourth book with the only difference visible in the image being that the badges are now diamond colored.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1cd9d3dcab3935f37d2ade8f30a4b22f3906b367.png)

_In the image above, A level I enchanted book can be seen on the left with the level increasing until it reaches level V on the right, with this being the maximum level available for certain books in vanilla survival Minecraft. In game, all level 5 enchanted books are animated with a pulsing aura._

### Enchanted Book Type
The majority of books in this pack derive their most basic texture elements from predictable base templates for different book types. These templates are split into two major parts, book color, and ribbon color. Each have many variants with looks based on enchantment features like, armor enchantment books, weapon enchantment books, tool enchantment books, or cursed books.

![This image displays a table of all basic templates used by every enchanted book in this pack, as well as title text displaying the words “Book & Ribbon Types”. On the left is a labeled column of undecorated book textures, while on the right is a labeled column of undecorated ribbon textures. Both columns start with the classic minecraft book and ribbon textures, both labeled as “Normal”. Starting at the top of the left column and continuing to the bottom of the left column. The first book is a vibrant dark blue color labeled as the base template for protection enchanted books. The second book is a dull dark blue color labeled as the base template for armor enchanted books. The third book is a dull dark brown color labeled as the base template for melee enchanted books. The fourth book is a vibrant dark red color labeled as the base template for bow enchanted books. The fifth book is a dull dark green color labeled as the base template for crossbow enchanted books. The sixth book is a prismarine colored book labeled as the base template for trident enchanted books. The seventh book is a dull blueish-gray color labeled as the base template for mace enchanted books. The eighth book is a gray colored book labeled as the base template for mining tool enchanted books. The ninth book is a dark turquoise color labeled as the base template for fishing enchanted books. The last book is a deep purplish-red colored book labeled as the base template for cursed enchanted books. Starting at the top of the right column and continuing to the bottom of the right column. The first ribbon is a gray colored ribbon labeled as the base template for miscellaneous enchanted books. The second ribbon is a white colored ribbon labeled as the base template for armor enchanted books. The third ribbon is a dark red color labeled as the base template for melee enchanted books. The fourth ribbon is a green color labeled as the base template for ranged enchanted books. The fifth ribbon is a brown color labeled as the base template for misc weapon enchanted books. The sixth ribbon is a yellow color labeled as the base template for mining tool enchanted books. The seventh ribbon is a vibrant blue color labeled as the base template for fishing enchanted books. The eighth ribbon is a dull orange color labeled as the base template for fire based enchanted books. The final ribbon is a bright orangish-red color labeled as the base template for explosive based enchanted books.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/2e103e7fb44ad87bc92225ea90d187aebee41484.png)

_In the image above, The default book and ribbon colors can be seen at the top of the chart. The lower section displays the different major book and ribbon templates with some minor ribbon templates also being displayed in the bottom right._

### The Final Product
After adding the components of the basic book and ribbon textures together, as well as taking the time to make each different enchantment book look distinct and unique. Then making a texture variant for each standardly available level of each respective enchanted book, We now finally have a unique texture for every basic enchanted book in vanilla Minecraft.

![This image displays every unique enchanted book texture for the 1.0 release of Asa’s Enchanted Books by SimplyAsa.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/3ae4b4aca60944ac45121d9d34b9fe9f1ebe7375.png)

_In the image above, All implemented book textures for the 1.0 release of this pack are visible. Can you guess which textures go to each enchantment?_

It is worth mentioning that some book textures are animated and so cannot be properly displayed in this image alone. All books with animated textures display only their first frame in these images, however, they should play as normal in-game.

## The Future of Asa's Enchanted Books
I plan on keeping this pack up to date with the latest (full release) Minecraft versions. However, new pack versions may not be available immediately after the release of new Minecraft updates as I work at my own pace. I also fully intend on making this pack compatible with certain mods, as well as a bare bones version of this texture pack since it is similar to the pack used in the official Minecraft promotional videos. I may also eventually make a 32x resolution version of this pack if there is enough interest.

## Currently Supported Enchanted Books
Minecraft : 1.21.5+

## Modded Books Coming Soon
MC Dungeons Weapons\
MC Dungeons Armors\
RPG Series Mods

## Alternative Texture Pack Compatibility Coming Soon
Bare Bones\
32x (eventually)
